subject: [RFC PATCH 25/40] mm: trigger deferred SPB evac when atomic allocs would taint a clean SPB

2026-05-20.patch_summary: This patch introduces a mechanism to trigger deferred superpageblock (SPB) evacuation when an atomic allocation would otherwise taint a clean SPB. The goal is to prevent the tainting of clean SPBs by allowing the next allocation to claim from the tainted pool instead. This is achieved by scheduling a deferred spb_evacuate_for_order() on the zone's pgdat workqueue whenever a non-movable allocation attempts to claim a pageblock from an empty or clean superpageblock as a fallback. The trigger is gated by single-flight, throttle, and tainted-pool precheck inside queue_spb_evacuate(), making it safe to fire from this hot path without storming the workqueue.
